block.createaddon.guidance_computer.tool: Collects values from nearby guidance sensors and exposes control commands for closed-loop contraptions.
Subscribes to every ISensor it can scan and reads their double, Vec3 or boolean value providers.
Combines terrain altitude, bearing error, drift, closing speed and structural stress into elevator, rudder and throttle commands.
Open the Ponder scene for a mechanical overview of the sensor bus.
block.createaddon.relative_bearing_senso: Measures the signed yaw error between this contraption and a target point or entity.
Returns -180 to +180 degrees. 0 means the craft is pointed directly at the target.
Useful for auto-rudder steering, turret aiming and antenna alignment.
block.createaddon.closing_speed_sensor.t: Measures relative approach speed along the line-of-sight to a target.
Returns metres per second. Positive values are closing, negative values are moving away.
Useful for terminal braking, docking governors and fuze prediction.
block.createaddon.terrain_altimeter.tool: A copper-faced sounding gauge that measures clearance below the contraption.
Casts downward and reports distance to the first collidable surface.
Reports height above the dimension sea level for marine and airship controllers.
Compares desired heading against actual horizontal movement to expose sideslip.
Returns a signed drift angle in degrees.
Useful for damping sliding ships, airships and hovercraft.
block.createaddon.lock_on_probability_se: Estimates target quality from range, angle and lateral target speed.
Returns a probability from 0 to 1 instead of a hard redstone-like lock.
Useful for launch authorization, fire-control logic and automatic interceptors.
block.createaddon.structural_stress_sens: Monitors mechanical and inertial load to protect fragile moving contraptions.
Returns a 0 to 1 danger level based on network load, velocity, rotation and acceleration spikes.
Useful for overload cutoffs, automatic slowdown and safe-mode triggers.
block.createaddon.relative_velocity_vect: Outputs the full target-relative velocity vector for advanced navigation math.
Returns target velocity minus own velocity as a Vec3, preserving lateral and vertical motion.
Useful for proportional navigation, lead pursuit and docking controllers.
block.createaddon.laser_range_finder.too: A brass-barrelled distance gauge whose range increases with rotational speed.
Max range = Speed × 0.5 blocks. At 256 RPM the beam reaches 128 blocks.
Useful for altitude hold, terrain profiling and closing-speed validation.
Rotating-dish scanner that locates the nearest entity or block coordinate.
Returns the target position as a Vec3. Right-click to cycle through scan modes.
Useful for radar-guided lock-on, waypoint tracking and area surveillance.
block.createaddon.testblock.tooltip.summ: A development kinetic block for checking shaft behavior and local Create integration.
block.createaddon.tin_ore.tooltip.summar: Early ore used for machine casings, printed parts and computation-era materials.
block.createaddon.deepslate_tin_ore.tool: A deeper tin source for extending early machine production underground.
block.createaddon.deepslate_tungsten_ore: A late, hard ore used for precision and high-temperature computation parts.
item.createaddon.raw_tin.tooltip.summary: Smelt or blast into tin ingots for early computation materials.
item.createaddon.tin_ingot.tooltip.summa: A soft metal ingredient for mechanical computation parts and early semiconductor tools.
item.createaddon.raw_tungsten.tooltip.su: Refine into tungsten for durable late-stage machine components.
A dense metal for high-temperature and precision computation machinery.
block.createaddon.crystal_pull_furnace.t: Grows raw silicon into a single crystal boule for wafer production.
Right-click to pull one silicon boule from the furnace.
block.createaddon.wafer_saw.tooltip.summ: Slices a silicon boule into multiple rough wafers.
block.createaddon.wafer_saw.tooltip.beha: Right-click to cut it into rough wafers.
block.createaddon.wafer_polisher.tooltip: Timed clean-room machine that polishes rough wafers before oxidation.
The wafer is inserted, visibly spins while processing, then can be extracted empty-handed.
block.createaddon.oxidation_diffusion_fu: Grows an oxide layer on a polished wafer for later lithography.
Right-click to produce an oxidized wafer.
block.createaddon.photolithography_table: Coats and exposes wafers as the first practical lithography station.
block.createaddon.packaging_test_bench.t: Finishes dies into packaged logic chips and prepares them for machine use.
item.createaddon.silica_dust.tooltip.sum: The mineral starting point for raw silicon and later wafer processing.
item.createaddon.raw_silicon.tooltip.sum: Feedstock for crystal pulling and the beginning of semiconductor computation.
item.createaddon.silicon_boule.tooltip.s: A pulled crystal ingot ready to be sliced into wafers.
item.createaddon.rough_wafer.tooltip.sum: Freshly sliced wafer blank that must be polished before precision work.
A flat wafer surface ready for oxidation and lithography.
A wafer with a grown oxide layer, ready for photoresist.
item.createaddon.photoresist_wafer.toolt: A coated wafer ready for mask exposure.
item.createaddon.etched_wafer.tooltip.su: An exposed wafer with early circuit patterns cut into the layer.
item.createaddon.doped_wafer.tooltip.sum: A wafer whose electrical regions have been altered for logic behavior.
item.createaddon.metallized_wafer.toolti: A wafer with conductive paths applied to connect logic regions.
Completed wafer material ready to be cut into logic dies.
Unpackaged logic die produced from the wafer chain.
item.createaddon.ceramic_chip_package.to: Protective package for turning fragile dies into usable chips.
item.createaddon.basic_logic_chip.toolti: The first compact electronic computation component in the tech tree.
item.createaddon.blank_table_paper.toolt: Input sheet for a table printer to produce processing curve data.
item.createaddon.basic_processing_curve_: Printed difference-engine output used by analytical machinery.
item.createaddon.blank_punched_card.tool: A blank program medium for future analytical-engine control logic.
item.createaddon.machine_calibration_car: Analytical-engine output intended to improve later industrial machines.
Difference-engine component that defines the function being tabulated.
block.createaddon.difference_register_co: Difference-engine component that stores mechanical increments for table printing.
Assembles nearby difference-engine parts to print a processing curve table.
Needs one function setting drum and two difference register columns within 4 blocks.
Starts timed computation; empty-hand right-click retrieves the finished table.
block.createaddon.punched_card_reader.to: Analytical-engine component that represents program input.
Analytical-engine component that represents the calculating Mill.
block.createaddon.store_memory_column.to: Analytical-engine component that represents mechanical memory storage.
block.createaddon.mechanical_printer.too: Assembles analytical-engine parts to compile a machine calibration card.
Needs one card reader, one Mill and one Store within 4 blocks.
Starts timed computation; empty-hand right-click retrieves the calibration card.
item.createaddon.sensor_binder.tooltip.s: Links a sensor block to a threshold controller without opening a GUI.
gui.createaddon.guidance_debug.hold_shif: Hold Shift to inspect sensor readings
gui.createaddon.guidance_debug.status.no: status: no target; target sensors need a designator/entity target
gui.createaddon.guidance_debug.status.ze: status: valid, but currently stationary/error is zero
Mechanical instruments for navigation, fire-control and closed-loop contraptions
createaddon.ponder.tag.sensor_inputs.des: Blocks that expose measured double, Vec3 or boolean values to a Guidance Computer
createaddon.ponder.tag.computation_chain: Mechanical computation, punched cards and calibration data for later machines
createaddon.ponder.tag.semiconductor_cha: Material preparation and wafer processing for electronic computation
createaddon.ponder.guidance_computer.tex: The Guidance Computer scans nearby sensor blocks and subscribes to their IValueProvider outputs.
A terrain-following loop can combine altitude, bearing, drift, closing speed and stress into rudder, elevator and throttle commands.
createaddon.ponder.relative_bearing_sens: Compares the craft's current heading with the target line-of-sight.
Output is yaw error in degrees: -180 to +180, where 0 means directly aligned.
Use it for automatic rudders, turret drives and antenna pointing.
Projects relative velocity onto the line-of-sight to a target.
Positive values mean the target is closing; negative values mean separation is increasing.
Use it for terminal braking, fuze timing and docking approach governors.
createaddon.ponder.terrain_altimeter.tex: Casts a mechanical sounding ray downward from the instrument body.
Terrain mode measures clearance to the first collision below; sea-level mode reports height above sea level.
Use it for terrain following, sea skimming and safe hover limits.
createaddon.ponder.inertial_drift_sensor: Compares desired heading with the actual horizontal displacement direction.
The output is a signed sideslip angle, useful even when the craft points one way and slides another.
Use it to damp airship drift, ship skid and lateral overshoot.
createaddon.ponder.lock_on_probability_s: Combines distance, angular error and lateral target speed into a single fire-control score.
The output ranges from 0 to 1 and deliberately remains a probability, not a hard yes/no switch.
Use it to gate launchers, cannons and interceptor logic.
createaddon.ponder.structural_stress_sen: Watches kinetic network load, linear speed, shaft speed and acceleration spikes.
The output is a 0 to 1 danger level, independent from ordinary speedometers.
Use it for overload protection, automatic throttling and safe-mode cutoffs.
createaddon.ponder.relative_velocity_vec: Exposes the complete target velocity minus craft velocity as a Vec3.
Unlike closing speed, this preserves lateral and vertical components for advanced interception math.
Use it for proportional navigation, lead pursuit and docking controllers.
createaddon.ponder.laser_range_finder.te: Fires a beam along the sensor axis whose range is proportional to input rotational speed.
At maximum Create speed (256 RPM) the beam reaches 128 blocks. Slower speeds shorten the reach.
Use it for terrain profiling, altitude hold and multi-sensor closing-speed validation.
createaddon.ponder.radar_indexer.text_0: Rotating-dish scanner that sweeps the area for entities or block coordinates.
createaddon.ponder.radar_indexer.text_1: Default mode returns the nearest living entity position as a Vec3. Right-click to cycle modes.
createaddon.ponder.radar_indexer.text_2: Use it for radar-guided lock-on, waypoint tracking and area surveillance.
block.createaddon.threshold_controller.t: Reads a nearby sensor and outputs a redstone signal when the value meets a threshold condition.
Cycle through available sensors within 6 blocks.
Send a number in chat to set the threshold value. Outputs redstone 15 when condition is met.
block.createaddon.rotary_frame.tooltip.s: A configurable frame that renders a filled block at arbitrary yaw and pitch angles.
block.createaddon.rotary_frame.tooltip.b: Fill the frame with that block's appearance.
Right-click rotates yaw. Sneak right-click rotates pitch.
item.createaddon.sensor_binder.tooltip.e: Right-click a sensor, then right-click a threshold controller.
message.createaddon.sensor_binder.missin: Stored sensor is missing or changed
message.createaddon.sensor_binder.wrong_: Stored sensor is in another dimension
message.createaddon.sensor_binder.invali: This block cannot receive sensor bindings
message.createaddon.machine.crystal_pull: Silicon boule pulled from seed crystal
message.createaddon.computation.started_: Difference engine started printing a processing curve table
Analytical engine started compiling a machine calibration card
message.createaddon.computation.busy: The computation assembly is still processing, or its output is waiting
block.createaddon.guidance_controller.to: A kinetic command relay that reads flight-control commands from a Guidance Computer and broadcasts them to nearby Servo Mounts.
Must receive rotational speed to function. When unpowered, all output commands are zero.
block.createaddon.servo_mount.tooltip.su: A mechanical actuator mount that reads a command channel and directly drives the angle of an adjacent bearing or the offset of an adjacent piston.
block.createaddon.servo_mount.tooltip.be: Cycle through command channels: Rudder, Elevator, Throttle.
Cycle angle range preset or toggle signal inversion.
